narration: Sixty-three percent of workers never negotiate their salary — not once. That silence has a price tag, and it'll make you sick to hear it.
visual: Person silhouette standing at a desk on dark background, large $1,500,000 counter animating up from zero in green text

narration: Here's number one: every job offer you see is a floor, not a ceiling. Companies almost always budget five to ten thousand above what they first offer — and they're counting on you to just say yes.
visual: Two side-by-side bars on light background: shorter 'THEIR OFFER $65K' vs taller 'REAL BUDGET $75K' with a $5K-$10K gap badge

narration: Number two: every raise you get is a percentage of that starting number. Accept a low first offer, and you're stuck compounding on a smaller base for the rest of your career.
visual: Two diverging salary lines on dark background — green line climbing steeply labeled NEGOTIATED, faded white line barely rising labeled SILENT

narration: That five thousand dollars you left on the table at twenty-five — at seven percent compounded for forty years — turns into $634,000. One conversation. Six figures.
visual: Animated stack of green coins on light background, counter ticking up from $5,000 to $634,000 with a progress bar

narration: Over a full forty-year career, consistent negotiators earn one point five million dollars more than those who never ask. Bonuses, 401k matches — all calculated off the number you were afraid to challenge.
visual: Two piggy banks side by side on dark background — large green one labeled NEGOTIATED $2.5M+, small faded one labeled SILENT $1M, with $1.5M DIFFERENCE badge

narration: Before your next review, look up your market rate and ask for ten percent above it. The worst answer is no — but silence already cost you a million and a half.
visual: Phone showing salary market rate data, three action cards sliding in: LOOK UP YOUR MARKET RATE, ASK 10% ABOVE THAT, WORST THEY SAY IS NO (highlighted in green)
